items.headline: Mixed Sentiment as Earnings Season Kicks Off Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: U.S. equities saw a mixed performance today as investors weighed the start of a busy Q2 earnings season against persistent geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which kept oil prices elevated. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq ended higher, while the Dow Jones also posted a gain, reflecting a cautious yet optimistic market sentiment driven by strong corporate profit expectations, particularly in the AI-driven technology sector.

items.headline: Aardvark Therapeutics Surges on Strong Day
items.summary: Aardvark Therapeutics experienced a significant surge, leading the market's gainers on a day of otherwise modest index movements.

items.headline: SUNation Energy Posts Solid Gains
items.summary: SUNation Energy continued its upward trend, making it one of the top performers today.

items.headline: Rackspace Technology Climbs
items.summary: Rackspace Technology saw a notable increase in its stock price today.

items.headline: SOBR Safe Declines Sharply
items.summary: SOBR Safe, Inc. experienced a significant downturn, making it one of the day's biggest losers.

items.headline: Catheter Precision Falls
items.summary: Catheter Precision, Inc. shares fell sharply today.

items.headline: Silo Pharma Experiences Notable Drop
items.summary: Silo Pharma, Inc. was among the top decliners in today's trading session.

items.headline: Inflation Data and Fed Outlook Ahead
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting key U.S. inflation data this week, with the Consumer Price Index (CPI) for June scheduled for release on Tuesday, July 14, and the Producer Price Index (PPI) on Wednesday, July 15. The market is also closely watching for any new signals regarding the Federal Reserve's policy outlook, with a 68.5% likelihood of unchanged interest rates in July's meeting.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Gains Momentum with Major Banks and Tech Reporting
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season is in full swing, with major U.S. banks including JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo set to report their results this week. Technology and AI-related companies like Netflix, UnitedHealth Group, Intuitive Surgical, and ASML are also scheduled to release their quarterly figures, with S&P 500 companies broadly expected to show a 24% increase in profits.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Tensions Drive Oil Prices Higher, Influence Market Sentiment
items.summary: Renewed military exchanges between the United States and Iran have led to a significant jump in crude oil prices and contributed to a cautious market sentiment. Geopolitical risks, particularly concerning shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, remain a key focus for investors, though some analysts note a 'muted reaction' to the renewed conflict.

items.headline: Technology and Energy Lead, Healthcare Lags in Sector Performance
items.summary: The technology and energy sectors demonstrated strong performance today, with semiconductors continuing their advance, partly due to AI-driven demand and rising crude oil prices. Conversely, the Health Care Select Sector SPDR (XLV) lagged, along with some metal and aviation stocks in the Indian market.
